summary: CC-0013 implements the KeystoneReconciler with 6 sequential sub-reconcilers (secrets, database, fernet, config, deployment, bootstrap), wired into main.go with external scheme registration. The review #1 feedback from greptile-apps[bot] has been fully addressed: EventRecorder type corrected to record.EventRecorder, sub-reconciler guards fixed to use !result.IsZero(), Fernet key encoding changed to include standard padding, plugin config merge order corrected, DB URL encoding refactored to use url.UserPassword for RFC 3986 compliance, FernetKeysReady fall-through fixed with early return, credential-keys volume made optional, and main.go method call fixed to GetEventRecorderFor. All 54 tests pass, go vet reports 0 issues, golangci-lint reports 0 issues, gosec reports no security findings.

tests_checklist.item: Orchestration tests cover CR not found, early return, Ready aggregation, sequential progression, idempotency, ObservedGeneration tracking
Each sub-reconciler has dedicated test file with happy path, not-ready requeue, error propagation, and condition message tests
Edge cases covered: stale Job detection, min active keys floor, special characters in DB credentials, brownfield mode skips MariaDB CRs
Error cases tested: API server errors (interceptor-injected), ErrJobFailed sentinel, missing ExternalSecrets
Tests use gomega with NewGomegaWithT(t) per mandatory pattern
Test assertions are unconditional (no vacuous assertions behind conditional guards)

code_quality_checklist.item: One file per sub-reconciler following project conventions
Functions are focused and small (single responsibility)
No code duplication — all shared logic delegated to internal/common/ libraries
No dead code or commented-out code

security_checklist.item: Fernet keys use crypto/rand for secure randomness (32 bytes)
DB credentials read via secrets.GetSecretValue, never hardcoded
URL encoding uses url.UserPassword for RFC 3986 userinfo compliance
No secrets logged or exposed in error messages

architecture_checklist.item: Sequential sub-reconciler chain with early return on !result.IsZero() || err != nil
Ready condition aggregated via conditions.AllTrue over 5 sub-conditions
main.go follows operator entrypoint via shared bootstrap pattern
External schemes (mariadb, ESO v1alpha1, ESO v1beta1) registered in init()
Owner references set via shared library functions (SetControllerReference)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No unnecessary abstractions — sub-reconcilers are direct methods on KeystoneReconciler
Excluded scope items (federation, TLS, HPA, metrics) are not implemented
Build functions are simple struct construction, no premature generalization

fail_fast_checklist.item: Sub-reconcilers return early with requeue on not-ready state
Errors wrapped with context at each level (fmt.Errorf with %w)
FernetKeysReady condition set to False with Requeue:true on initial creation (fall-through fixed)

defensive_checklist.item: Fernet key count floored at 3 even if MaxActiveKeys < 3
Credential-keys volume made Optional=true for graceful degradation
Database port defaults to 3306 if not set

issues_found.description: TODO comment remains in final output: 'TODO(CC-0013): Credential key management is deferred to a future feature.' This was flagged by the stub scanner in pre-validation. However, this is a documented, intentional deferral with a working workaround (Optional=true) and the feature is explicitly listed in the Excluded scope. The TODO serves as a tracking marker for the future feature, not a stub.
issues_found.fix: No action required — the TODO is acceptable as a forward reference for a future feature that is explicitly out of scope.

issues_found.description: REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT comment explains why url.UserPassword was chosen over url.PathEscape. The technical rationale is correct (url.PathEscape does not escape '@' or ':' which are delimiters in RFC 3986 userinfo). The comment style ('bitte prüfen') mixes languages.
issues_found.fix: Consider removing the 'bitte prüfen' suffix or translating to English for consistency with the English-only codebase convention.

suggested_improvements: Consider adding RBAC marker for events resource (groups=core,resources=events,verbs=create;patch) since record.EventRecorder creates Event objects. Currently this may work via default controller-manager ClusterRole, but explicit RBAC markers are the recommended practice for kubebuilder-generated RBAC manifests. This is a pre-existing gap (not introduced by the current diff).
The 'bitte prüfen' text in the REVIEWER-DISAGREEMENT comment at reconcile_config.go:83 should be in English per the LANGUAGE critical rule.
next_steps: Integration/E2E tests (CC-0014) as documented in Excluded scope
Credential key management sub-reconciler (tracked by TODO at reconcile_deployment.go:153)
title: F001: Implement Keystone reconciler and sub-reconcilers
